This app enables MS Graph API-based email ingestion and investigative actions on Microsoft 365, replacing the Microsoft Graph for Office 365 app.
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: Supporting Add-on for CUBE - v0.5.1 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

-- First public release


Description:
This app provides the index-time configuration to ingest the CDR data from Cisco Unified Border Element aka "CUBE". It is designed to be used in conjunction with Sideview's "SA_cube" app, aka "Supporting App for CUBE", deployed on your Search Head tier.
item.dc:creator: Sideview, LLC - Partner

item.title: Supporting App for CUBE - v0.5.1 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

-- First public release


Description:
This app implements various field extraction configuration and Sideview conventions so as to allow CDR and also syslog data from CUBE to be not only searched in Splunk effectively, but also to be pulled into Sideview's commercial "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics" solution and investigated and analyzed there. NOTE that this Supporting App is designed to be always deployed with Sideview's "Supporting Add-on for CUBE" ALSO deployed on the Indexing and/or Forwarding tiers. That "Add-on" component contains crucial index-time configuration to index the data into Splunk correctly, and the index-time configuration in that TA is NOT duplicated in this app here. Therefore if you intend to set up a standalone deployment you will need both the TA and the SA. These two apps are furthermore designed to work with Sideview's commercial "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics" app. When all three components are present and deployed to the right tiers, users can navigate, troubleshoot and build ad-hoc charts and reports around your CUBE data, within the the Cisco CDR app's complex user interfaces.
item.dc:creator: Sideview, LLC - Partner

item.title: TrackMe - v2.3.18 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

TrackMe 2.3.18 — Virtual Groups, Policy Enhancements & Performance Optimizations

  • Virtual Groups — cross-tenant aggregation views: This release introduces Virtual Groups, a powerful new feature for the Virtual Tenants UI. Virtual Groups are read-only cards that aggregate entities from multiple tenants and components into a single unified view, displayed alongside regular tenant cards. Combined with the recently introduced priority and tags policies — both lookup-based and search-based — and a new lightweight entity filter DSL, Virtual Groups enable a high level of flexibility in representing and monitoring key entities across tenants. A full creation wizard, RBAC-controlled visibility, and simulation mode make it easy to define, preview, and manage groups.
  • Key performance optimizations: The FLX converging tracker has been re-engineered with direct REST calls and parallel execution, delivering up to 6-10x faster execution and eliminating excessive skip rates. The WLK metadata tracker received connection caching and batch KV loading optimizations for similar ~6-10x performance gains at large scale.
  • Various bug fixes and improvements: Multiple fixes across DHM policies, table rendering, input sanitization, and policy simulation improve reliability and usability across the platform.

See: https://docs.trackme-solutions.com/latest/releasenotes.html#version-2-3-18-build-1774997466-01-04-2026


Description:
TrackMe for Splunk provides visibility and operational excellence to monitor at scale your Splunk data sources availability and quality, and many more. With a rich set of features and a powerful workflow, TrackMe empowers you day after day to get the most from your Splunk investments and deliver the five stars quality of service your users deserve. Discover TrackMe: https://trackme-solutions.com/discover Documentation: https://docs.trackme-solutions.com You can subscribe to our newsletter, and receive important communications from us. We will send notifications for instance when publishing new releases, so we can you let you know the cool and powerful things we've been working on! https://trackme-solutions.com/subscribe/ TrackMe has transitioned to a new licensing model. The Free Community Edition has been discontinued. What this means for you: - Existing licensed customers are not impacted. Your current license continues unchanged. - Community users are automatically upgraded to a Foundation Edition trial for 90 days, with full functionality enabled. After the trial period: - TrackMe remains active and fully usable for monitoring - The platform enters read-only mode You can continue to view data, dashboards, alerts, and history Creation of new tenants, trackers, or entities will be disabled To continue using TrackMe with full capabilities after the trial, a Foundation license is required. Buy Foundation Edition: https://trackme-solutions.com/get-foundation/ See all plans: https://trackme-solutions.com/pricing/
item.dc:creator: Trackme Limited

item.title: PAVO Getwatchlist Add-on - v1.4.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Version 1.4.0

  • Enhanced Error handling for more graceful and information error messages

  • Added Credential Management for Profiles.

  • Graphical Interface for profiles

  • Reviewed default profiles for the correct operation of settings

  • Various bug fixes

  • Validated for Splunk Python 3.13

  • Added a file:// support.

  • JAILED: This input is jailed to the $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/getwatchlist/watchlists directory.


Description:
Getwatchlist is a custom search command for Splunk which will return a CSV formatted list from a URL. This is useful for creating lookup tables and keeping them up to date from external or internal sources. These watchlists can contain virtually anything such as domain names, IP addresses, host names, email addresses, filenames, etc. and can then be used in searches against your events. These watchlists can be in any delimited format (tab, comma, space, etc.).
item.dc:creator: Aplura LLC

item.title: Content Pack for SNMP and NetFlow - v1.0.17 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

New Features

- Automated Network Device Discovery:  ITSI now automatically discovers and categorizes network devices based on the device_type Info field, streamlining asset integration and reducing manual configuration.

Enhancements

- Enriched Device Metadata:  The device_type attribute has been added as a standard Info field across the content pack, providing consistent classification and improved granularity for service health scores and entity filtering.


Short Description:
Pre-built network monitoring for Splunk ITSI and ITEW — automatic device discovery, interface health, and SNMP trap alerting powered by NetFlow Optimizer.


Description:
Built by NetFlow Logic, an official Splunk Partner — this Content Pack brings pre-built network monitoring directly into Splunk IT Service Intelligence (ITSI) and IT Essentials Work (ITEW). Powered by NetFlow Optimizer (NFO), it automatically discovers network devices and interfaces, monitors their health in real time, and delivers proactive alerting via SNMP traps — all out of the box, with no manual entity configuration required. What this Content Pack gives you: - Automatic device and interface discovery — network devices and interfaces are imported as ITSI/ITEW entities without manual configuration - Real-time health monitoring — track CPU utilisation, memory usage, interface load, and errors/discards per device and interface - Proactive SNMP trap alerting — receive and correlate real-time network event alerts for faster incident response - Service-based visibility — five pre-built ITSI services for Network Firewall, Router, Switch, Load Balancer, and Interface Health — each with dedicated KPIs - Pre-built dashboards — covering interface errors and discards, network conversations (by traffic, application, user, country, protocol, and autonomous system), cyber threats and top violators, and SNMP device CPU and memory health Requirements: - NetFlow Optimizer (NFO) with SNMP monitoring enabled. - Requires Splunk ITSI or IT Essentials Work (ITEW). - The Technology Add-on for NetFlow (TA-netflow)(https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1838/) must also be installed. Start a free 60-day trial of NetFlow Optimizer at netflowlogic.com/free-trial — or request a demo at netflowlogic.com/request-a-demo.
item.dc:creator: NetFlow Logic

item.title: Dark Web Scan by Bechtle App - v1.1.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Bug fixes and stability improvements. This update is recommended for all users.


Short Description:
See if your company's data was leaked to the dark web with this app for the Dark Web Scan by Bechtle.


Description:
See if your company's data was leaked to the dark web with this app for the Dark Web Scan by Bechtle. Credentials of your employees, customers, suppliers and service technicians get stolen by cybercriminals and are sold on the dark web. Get notified about any new offers or leaks regarding your company using this app. - We permanently look out for new forums, marketplaces or chat groups and get access. - Then we crawl data from these sources on the digital underground and save it to our database. - You get notified if there's something about your company being sold. Using the custom Splunk search commands, you can even customize your automations. For example, you could set up your own automation to send an email to users with leaked credentials or reset their passwords automatically. (The automation requires additional setup and is not part of the app.) Additionally, the Dark Web Scan by Bechtle also allows you to search for common misconfigurations or other risks that could lead to security incidents in the future, e.g. email security settings, the security of your website against common cyberattacks or OSINT info like publicly discoverable email addresses or subdomains.
item.dc:creator: Bechtle Darkwebscan Team

item.title: Splunk Health Assistant Add-on - v2026.3.30 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Description:
The Splunk Health Assistant Add-on lets you download and install new health check items as they become available for the monitoring console in Splunk Enterprise. The add-on is updated with new health check items independently of Splunk Enterprise releases, which lets you update and monitor your deployment with the latest health checks, without waiting for a Splunk Enterprise release or requesting help from Splunk Support. You can download the the Splunk Health Assistant Add-on directly through the monitoring console UI. This installs the latest version of the add-on in $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps and automatically adds the new health checks to the Health Check page in the monitoring console. No restart is required. Or, you can manually download the add-on and install it using the app management page or command line if your splunk instance cannot access Splunkbase, for example, due to firewall restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks - v3.0.1 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Description:
The Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks lets you collect data from Cortex XDR, IoT Security, Firewalls, Panorama, and Strata Logging Service. The new Add-on provides a health check monitoring dashboard and CIM-compatible knowledge objects. Key Highlights of the release: Modular inputs for IoT Security & Cortex XDR CIM normalisation Health check monitoring dashboard Support for the latest PanOS Important information for users of the Palo Alto Networks-owned Add-on and App: Due to certain differences between the Palo Alto Networks-owned Add-on and the Splunk-supported Add-on, it's important to understand the key changes, such as revised CIM mapping, macro adjustments, and configuration updates for IoT Security and Cortex XDR inputs. Familiarizing yourself with these changes and their impact is crucial to ensuring a smooth migration to the Splunk-supported Add-on and App. More details can be found in the Migration section of the Add-on documentation. Documentation for this add-on is posted at [Splunk Docs](https://splunk.github.io/splunk-add-on-for-palo-alto-networks)
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: BeyondTrust Privilege Management Cloud - v26.1.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Added configurable values for connection and read timeouts to provide flexibility in environments with very large data sets in PM Cloud


Description:
For BeyondTrust Privileged Management Cloud customers seeking a simple solution to ingest event data into Splunk, this app utilizes REST API endpoints available on any PM Cloud instance to retrieve both client event data as well as activity audit data. These two sources can be configured and controlled individually, and the integration is simple to configure, requiring no additional components outside of PM Cloud, Splunk, and the add-on itself.
item.dc:creator: BeyondTrust Corporation

item.title: QualiBooth Digital Accessibility Intelligence for Splunk - v1.1.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Description:
QualiBooth Digital Accessibility Intelligence for Splunk integrates accessibility intelligence from QualiBooth directly into the Splunk platform, enabling organizations to operationalize digital accessibility monitoring within their observability and security workflows. By ingesting accessibility insights into Splunk, teams gain centralized visibility into compliance status, violations, and trends across their digital properties. This helps engineering, security, and compliance teams detect issues earlier, monitor accessibility posture continuously, and incorporate accessibility signals into existing dashboards, alerts, and reporting. • Bring Digital Accessibility Monitoring into Splunk • Turn Accessibility Issues into Actionable Observability Signals • Monitor Accessibility Compliance in Your SIEM
item.dc:creator: AIOPS Group Monitoring Team

item.title: Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics - v8.3.2 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

-- Functionality - The first panel in the "Extension Detail" page is now a more sophisticated dynamic table, with a field picker and many available fields. Most particularly the user can select any of the "leg_type" definitions they may have created, and for each it will display the number of calls that have one or more legs matching those leg_types.
-- Functionality - The "Reports" tab on the App's landing page now allows users to delete or disable reports for which they have write permissions. It also allows them to convert Saved Reports to Alerts, allowing a subset of the full Alerting and Trigger options. For comprehensive Alert creation they will still use the "Edit Report (splunk ui)" option.
-- Fixed a bug in the "Reports" tab on the App's landing page, where Scheduled Reports and Alerts were never marked with "yes" in the "Scheduled" column.
-- Fixed a bug in "Investigate Devices" when 'include devices with zero calls' was enabled, where search terms like 'calls=0' would not work as filters and would instead return zero results.


Description:
This commercial app is available as a 60 day trial and is compatible with both Splunk Enterprise and Splunk Cloud. The app gives your team greatly increased visibility, monitoring, and reporting functionality around Cisco Unified Communications (aka CUCM / CallManager), whether you're a small organization with a hundred phones or one of the largest companies in the world. Whether troubleshooting individual calls or building high level reporting, it helps you visualize and understand in all areas: agents, users, and huntgroups; concurrency and utilization; call flow; call quality; call volume; devices; failed calls; locations and sites; and audits and compliance. Your team can trust it as their day-to-day tool for navigating call data from your Cisco CallManager deployment, and it can also become their main tool for building custom reports, alerts and dashboards around that data. This is true whether it’s a standard report you’d expect or a custom report that nothing else has been able to provide. Note that this isn't a package of content, but more a tank that allows you to drive around in your data. You won’t find a leftnav full of canned CDR reports to click on (that "don't quite fit"). Instead, you get an open flexible framework to investigate anything, drill down into anything, report on anything, over anything, by anything (occasionally with hands-on help from Sideview Support =). It is available as a free 60-day trial, and you can download and set it up today in less than 30 minutes.
item.dc:creator: Sideview, LLC - Partner

item.title: Sideview Supporting App for Webex - v0.6.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

-- Fixed a typo in the INGEST_EVAL for the call_id field.
-- The landing page now checks whether the Cisco CDR app is installed but also whether the webex data type is actually enabled there. If so the drilldown context menus present options to take the user there for further investigations.
-- Added a health check to verify that the custom_index macro in this app is listed in the macro of the same name over in the broader cisco_cdr app.


Description:
This Supporting App adapts data indexed via Splunk's "Cisco Webex Add-on for Splunk" allowing users to leverage that data within Sideview's commercial app "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics".
item.dc:creator: Sideview, LLC - Partner

item.title: Supporting App for Oracle SBC CDR - v0.8.2 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

-- Some charts on the app's homepage now have drilldown available. First the app detects whether the cisco_cdr app is also installed, and also whether it's installed but the admins haven't yet enabled the oracle SBC data type within. Depending on the answer there are different drilldown options available.
-- Fixed a broken URL in one of the links in the app's top right help menu.


Description:
This app provides the Splunk configuration to ingest the CDR data from Oracle SBC. It is intended to work for both Oracle Session Router CDR and Oracle SBC CDR, although some unknowns remain as of this writing. It is also designed to work as a Supporting App alongside Sideview's commercial "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics" app. When used in conjunction with the Cisco CDR app, the Oracle SBC data can be used in that app alongside the CUCM data.
item.dc:creator: Sideview LLC

item.title: Supporting App for Expressway CDR - v0.5.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

-- Fixed two bugs in the drilldowns from the new homepage.
-- Fixed a bug around health check messaging - even if no health checks actually failed the intro text for the messages would appear.


Short Description:
This app implements various field extraction configuration and Sideview conventions so as to allow syslog-formatted Expressway CDR data to be not only searched in Splunk effectively, but also to be pulled into Sideview's commercial "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics" solution on Splunk and investigated and analyzed there.


Description:
This app implements various field extraction configuration and Sideview conventions so as to allow syslog-formatted Expressway CDR data to be not only searched in Splunk effectively, but also to be pulled into Sideview's commercial "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics" solution on Splunk and investigated and analyzed there. NOTE that this Supporting App is designed to be always deployed with Sideview's "Supporting Add-on for Expressway CDR" ALSO deployed on the Indexing tier. That "Add-on" component contains crucial index-time configuration to index the CDR into Splunk correctly, and that index-time configuration is NOT duplicated in this app here. Therefore if you intend to set up a standalone deployment you will need both the TA and the SA. These two apps are furthermore designed to work with Sideview's commercial "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics" app. When all three components are present and deployed to the right tiers, users can navigate, troubleshoot and build ad-hoc charts and reports around the Expressway data, within the the Cisco CDR app's complex user interfaces.
item.dc:creator: Sideview, LLC - Partner

item.title: GoAhead D3 SUNBURST - v1.2.2 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

No enhancement, just for avoiding the App archive automatically.


Description:
"D3 SUNBURST" is a custom viz App to show the sunburst chart by using "D3.js". This Sunburst chart has breadcrumbs of field name and field value and a description in central. 51 color scheme can be used and the sizes of breadcrumbs and central description color can be set by formatter. Drilldown and dashboard token are available for additional search. Collaborative development by "Mitsuhiro Nakamura" & "Tatsuya Hasegawa" in 'GoAhead Inc'. Enjoy your SUNBURST visualization !
item.dc:creator: GoAhead Dev Team

item.title: GoAhead D3 HORIZON - v1.1.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

No enhancement. Just modify the metadata in app.conf.


Description:
Custom visualization App to show horizon chart by using "D3.js". This horizon chart is more colorful and has overlap function of area. X axis is Datetime by default, however Numerical and Categorical fields are available. Also, Drilldown and dashboard token are available for additional search. Furthermore, custom tooltip is supported by moving the mouse cursol on the area. Let's compare the beautiful waves per category, this chart may possibly replace stacked area chart!
item.dc:creator: GoAhead Dev Team

item.title: Splunk Add-on for Linux - v2.1.1 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Fixed an issue where some Linux audit events did not keep their exact time down to the millisecond. Timestamps are now recorded with full precision, improving the accuracy of event timelines and correlation.


Description:
The Splunk Add-on for Linux allows a Splunk software administrator to collect Linux performance metrics using HTTP Event Collector (HEC) or TCP. The Splunk Add-on for Linux collects data includes: * CPU metrics. * Memory metrics. * Swap metrics. * Mountpoint usage/FS usage. * Network interface traffic. * Disk utilization. * System load. * Process information. * Network protocols information. * IRQ metrics. * TCP connections information. * Thermal information. * System uptime statistics.
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: Cribl Decrypt Add-On for Splunk - v2.1.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

  • Removed JS version of the command to retain Cloud Compatibility.
  • Both "cribldecrypt" and "cribldecryptv2" now run the Python version.
  • Download version 2.0.2 if you need the JS version instead of the Python version.


Description:
The Cribl Decrypt Add-On for Splunk provides a custom command - cribldecryptv2 - that will decrypt encrypted data ingested from Cribl at search time. Note: Version 2.0.0 and above of this command only works on Linux Search Heads. It will not work on Windows because Splunk does not ship the required Windows Python library.
item.dc:creator: nthº Solutions

item.title: Splunk DB Connect - v4.2.3 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

See Release Notes for Splunk DB Connect.


Description:
Warning: You can't upgrade DB Connect directly from versions earlier than 3.18.0 to versions 4.0.0 or later. Make sure to follow upgrade path guide. Splunk DB Connect is a generic SQL database extension for Splunk that enables easy integration of database information with Splunk queries and reports. Splunk DB Connect supports DB2/Linux, Informix, MemSQL, MySQL, AWS Aurora, Microsoft SQL Server, Oracle, PostgreSQL, AWS RedShift, SAP SQL Anywhere, Sybase ASE, Sybase IQ, Teradata, InfluxDB and MongoDB Atlas & Standalone. Use Splunk DB Connect's Inputs to import structured data for powerful indexing, analysis, and visualization. Use Outputs to export machine data insights to a legacy database to increase your organization's insight. Use Lookups to add meaningful information to your event data by referencing fields in an external database. Use query commands to build live dashboards mixing structured and unstructured data.
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: Microsoft SQL Server - v2.3.8 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: SOAR


Release Notes:

  • Added an encryption configuration parameter to control the TLS encryption mode for database connections;
  • fixed encryption not being applied due to a known pymssql bug (pymssql/pymssql#924) by configuring it via a FreeTDS config file instead


Description:
This app supports investigative actions against a Microsoft SQL Server
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: Cisco Security Cloud - v3.6.4 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Release Notes – Cisco Security Cloud Splunk App

Release Date: March 30, 2026

We are pleased to announce the latest release of the Cisco Security Cloud Splunk App. This update delivers new feature enhancements, compliance improvements, and important bug fixes aimed at improving stability, usability, and security across multiple modules. We encourage all users to update to this latest version.

✅ What's New – Enhancements

  • Test Automation: Expanded automated test coverage for Advanced Logging to improve reliability and quality assurance.
  • Cisco Security Cloud Support: Introduced support for the Cisco Security Cloud Splunk App integration.
  • Cisco Secure Email Threat Defense: Updated the Fed-Prod URL to ensure uninterrupted service for federal production environments.
  • Cisco XDR: Improved relay feedback integration for a more seamless XDR experience.
  • Version Management: Implemented an in-app version check to help administrators stay current with the latest releases.
  • Security & Compliance – Magic 8 Best Practices: Applied industry-aligned best practices across the following modules to enhance security posture and app performance:
    • CII
    • Endpoint
    • ETD
    • CVI
    • SMA
    • DUO

🐛 Bug Fixes

  • SMA Dashboard: Corrected a misaligned pop-over menu on the Recent Samples tile for improved navigation.
  • Advanced Logging Dashboard: Standardized tile title formatting for a consistent and professional appearance.
  • Shell Application:
    • Resolved an issue where the Alerts and Detection Source Type filter failed to clear all data as expected.
    • Fixed radio button display styling that caused buttons to render as ellipses instead of circles.
  • AI Defense: Fixed a missing Event Logs table title when no results were present, improving dashboard clarity.
  • Cisco Secure Endpoint: Resolved a KVStore exception triggered during input creation on Cloud instances, restoring reliable cloud deployment functionality.

📌 Note: For installation instructions, compatibility requirements, or support, please refer to the official Cisco documentation or contact your Cisco representative.


Description:
The Cisco Security Cloud application offers seamless integration for connecting your Cisco devices with Splunk. It features a modular UX input design, built-in health checks, and constant monitoring to ensure operational integrity. Product(s) Enabled: Cisco AI Defense Cisco Duo Cisco Email Threat Defense (ETD) Cisco Endpoint Visibility Module (EVM) CIM Mappings (Beta) Cisco Identity Intelligence (CII) Cisco Multicloud Defense Cisco NVM Cisco Secure Endpoint Cisco Secure Firewall (FTD/eStreamer/ASA) Cisco Secure Workload Cisco Isovalent Cisco Isovalent Edge Processor **Alpha Cisco Secure Malware Analytics (SMA) Cisco Secure Network Analytics (SNA) Cisco Vulnerability Intelligence Cisco XDR (Incident Import & Promote to ES Notable)
item.dc:creator: Cisco Systems, Inc.

item.title: Analyst1 - v1.5.8 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Splunk TA v1.5.8

Stability Improvements and Fixes

  • Fixed a pyOpenSSL/cryptography version mismatch that would result in the Analyst1 TA configuration page returning HTTP 500 ("Unable to xml-parse") errors. This issue only occurs on Splunk 9.x environments where the system cryptography library is older than version 41 and another installed app (e.g., Splunk_TA_aws) triggers pyOpenSSL to load during startup.

  • Resolved an issue that would result in an HTTP 500 ("Something went wrong! Configuration page failed to load") when attempting to load the Analyst1 TA configuration page. This issue could occur when the TA was installed on a different Splunk instance, deleted from a deployer and re-deployed to SHC members, migrated between environments, or credential entries become corrupted. The fix ensures invalid credential entries are handled safely instead of crashing, allowing accounts and data collection to function normally even if corrupted entries exist.

  • Addressed an issue where custom commands failed to access KVStore collections when run via scheduled searches or | savedsearch on Splunk Cloud 10.2 because searchinfo.app resolved to an empty string, resulting in invalid namespace paths and HTTP 404 errors. This issue occurs specifically in scheduler-dispatched contexts where app metadata is not properly populated.

  • Optimized Search Head Cluster performance. Reduced unnecessary processing on non-captain cluster members and improved the efficiency of configuration change detection across cluster nodes.


Description:
The Analyst1 App for Splunk is an add-on designed for use by existing Analyst1 customers. This add-on has two main functions: 1. Brings enrichment data around observables/indicators of compromise from Analyst1 into Splunk, providing lookup tables for correlation data. 2. Sends network/host log data from Splunk to Analyst1, correlating syslogs into hits against IOCs
item.dc:creator: Analyst1 Team

item.title: Dataminr Pulse for Splunk SOAR - v2.1.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: SOAR


Release Notes:

  • Added support for python 3.13


Description:
Pulse's AI-powered real-time intelligence integrates into Splunk SOAR workflows for faster detection and response
item.dc:creator: SOAR Community

item.title: PSTree for Splunk - v2.1.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Updated splunk-sdk-python to 2.1.1 version


Description:
This apps main function is to enable a custom Splunk search command to reconstruct a pstree from Sysmon process creation events (EventCode 1). Information from memory forensics, such as Volatility's pstree, can be very helpful to detect malicious processes. By ingesting Sysmon events in Splunk and using this command you can quickly get similar information without performing memory forensics.
item.dc:creator: Donald Murchison

item.title: Cisco Intersight Add-on for Splunk - v3.1.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

VERSION 3.1.1

  1. Corrected and improved the wording of the help text for the Intersight Hostname field in account configuration to reduce user confusion.
  2. Added backend handling to automatically remove the **“https://”** prefix (if provided) and store only the required domain value, preventing misconfiguration.


Description:
Cisco Intersight Add-on for Splunk is a Cisco supported extension to Splunk platform designed to integrate Cisco Intersight's powerful infrastructure management capabilities with Splunk's robust data analysis platform. This add-on enables IT administrators and engineers to collect, monitor, and analyze data from Cisco Intersight, providing deep insights into their data center operations and enhancing their ability to manage infrastructure efficiently. The Cisco Intersight Add-on for Splunk enables data collection from key sources including Alarms, Audit Logs, Inventory, and Metrics within the Cisco Intersight platform. It also includes pre-built dashboards that provide high-level insights into the collected data, allowing for faster analysis and streamlined monitoring. For more information: Please refer to the User Guide: https://intersight.com/help/saas/resources#intersight_add-on_for_splunk
item.dc:creator: Cisco Systems, Inc.

item.title: Maps+ for Splunk - v4.6.2 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Maps+ for Splunk — Release Notes v4.6.2

Bug Fixes

Fixed: Markers not refreshing on auto-refresh panels

In Simple XML dashboards using a <refresh> interval, maps would appear to update but markers, tooltips, and popup values would stay frozen at the values from the initial page load. After multiple refresh cycles, duplicate markers could also appear on the map.

This release fixes the issue completely. Markers and tooltips now update correctly on every refresh cycle, and the marker count stays consistent with the underlying search results.

The fix also resolves a related JavaScript crash that would occur on the first auto-refresh when clustering was enabled, leaving the panel blank after the initial render.


Description:
The mapping equivalent of a Swiss Army knife for Splunk.
item.dc:creator: Splunk Works

item.title: Flashpoint - v3.0.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: SOAR


Release Notes:

  • Added support for python 3.13


Description:
This app implements the investigative actions for the Flashpoint on the Phantom Platform
item.dc:creator: SOAR Community

item.title: Netskope - v4.1.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: SOAR


Release Notes:

  • Added support for Python 3.13


Description:
This app integrates with the Netskope to execute various investigative and polling actions
item.dc:creator: SOAR Community

item.title: Lansweeper - v1.1.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: SOAR


Release Notes:

  • Added support for Python 3.13


Description:
This app integrates with Lansweeper to perform investigative actions
item.dc:creator: SOAR Community

item.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows - v10.0.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

  • Removed python3.7 support
  • Removed duplicate EVAL-reason_id from [source::XmlWinEventLog:Security]
  • New mapping for field user in Event Id 5140


Description:
*** Important: Read upgrade instructions and test add-on update before deploying to production *** The Splunk Add-on for Windows 5.0.0 introduced breaking changes. If you are upgrading from a version of the Splunk Add-on for Windows that is earlier than 5.0.0, you must follow the documented upgrade instructions to avoid data loss. A best practice is to test the upgraded version in a non-production environment before deploying to production. Neither the Splunk Add-on for Windows DNS version 1.0.1 nor the Splunk Add-on for Windows Active Directory version 1.0.0 is supported when installed alongside the Splunk Add-on for Windows version 6.0.0. The Splunk Add-on for Windows version 6.0.0 includes the Splunk Add-on for Windows DNS and the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory. The Splunk for Microsoft Windows add-on includes predefined inputs to collect data from Windows systems and maps to normalize the data to the Common Information Model.
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: VMware Carbon Black Cloud - v2.2.6 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

VMware Carbon Black Cloud App for Splunk v2.2.6

Splunk 10 Compatibility

  • Set python.version = python3.9 across all modular inputs, REST endpoints, alert actions, and custom commands for full Splunk 10.x Python 3.9 runtime support
  • Minimum supported Splunk version is now 9.3 (9.2 and older no longer supported)

CBC SDK Update

  • Upgraded vendored CBC SDK from 1.5.9 to 1.5.10
  • Removed dependency on backports-datetime-fromisoformat C extension, which caused ModuleNotFoundError on Splunk 10.2's Python 3.9.25 runtime
  • Removed dead backports and rpds vendor directories containing non-portable compiled artifacts

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed proxy SSL configuration in alert actions and custom commands — is identity comparisons were silently preventing HTTPS proxy connections from being established
  • Added export = system to metadata/default.meta for datamodels, tags, transforms, props, eventtypes, views, savedsearches, and commands — resolves issue where the VMWare_CBC datamodel was not visible to Splunk's search subsystem
  • Removed leftover props.conf.orig file

Security Updates

  • Updated requests to 2.32.4 (CVE-2024-47081)
  • Updated urllib3 to 1.26.19 (CVE-2024-37891)
  • Updated certifi to 2024.7.4 (CVE-2024-39689)
  • Updated validators to 0.21.0 (CVE-2023-45813)


Description:
The VMware Carbon Black Cloud App for Splunk is a single application to integrate your endpoint and workload security features and telemetry directly into Splunk dashboards, workflows and alert streams. This application connects with any Carbon Black Cloud offering and replaces the existing product-specific Carbon Black apps for Splunk. This app provides a unified solution to integrate Carbon Black Cloud Endpoint and Workload offerings with Splunk Enterprise, Splunk Cloud, and Splunk Enterprise Security (ES). Out-of-the-box, this app provides holistic visibility into the state of your endpoints and workloads through customizable dashboards and alert feeds in Splunk. For distributed Splunk environments, please use the Input Add-on (https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5333/) and the Technology Add-on (https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5334/).
item.dc:creator: Carbon Black Developer Network

item.title: Censys ASM Add-on for Splunk Platform - v1.3.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Adding proxy parameter to the censysasmhosts command to allow a proxy to be used for that command.


Description:
The Censys Add-on for Splunk allows Censys ASM users to import Logbook data into Splunk®, where changes in their attack surface can be easily directed to downstream security and analytics applications.
item.dc:creator: Censys Inc

item.title: Supporting Add-on for Expressway CDR - v0.5.0 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

First public release


Short Description:
Specifies index-time settings to correctly ingest syslog-formatted CDR records from Cisco Expressway.


Description:
This Supporting Add-on defines index-time settings to ingest syslog-formatted CDR records from Cisco Expressway into Splunk. It is to be deployed on your Splunk Indexers (or if you are using a Heavy Forwarder and forwarding cooked data to your indexes, installed on that HF). It is most effective when also deployed with two other apps on the Splunk Search Head(s) or Search Head Cluster: a) Sideview's Supporting App for Cisco Expressway, and b) Sideview's commercial "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics" app. With these pieces in place the Sideview apps on the search head implement a general purpose investigation and reporting solution for this data. NOTE: There are also some references in the app's config to other sourcetypes cisco_expressway_cdr_csv and/or cisco_expressway_cdr_json. These are still under development.
item.dc:creator: Sideview, LLC - Partner

item.title: FortiGate - v2.3.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: SOAR


Release Notes:

  • Updated connector auth to use bearer token.


Description:
This app supports a variety of containment and investigative actions on the FortiGate Firewall
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: Canary - v2.1.3 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

-- Report module now allows an additional axis of reporting fields beyond "splitByField", named "alphaField".
-- Sideview's contextual menu pattern now allows a style for a 'menuDisabledLink' for apps that want to inform users that certain functionality exists even if their user account lacks necessary capabilities to use it.


Description:
Canary is essentially an alternate User Interface framework for Splunk. It implements its own extensible view creation slash dashboarding system that other apps can use. It is a descendant of the old Sideview Utils (defunct for years now) but whereas Sideview Utils ran on top of Splunk’s “Advanced XML” systems, Canary does not. For a great deal more information about Canary, see our website ( https://sideviewapps.com/documentation/canary ) NOTE: This app runs in python3 without any problems and without any changes required. Splunk's "Upgrade Readiness App" may erroneously state that our apps do not run in python3 but it is wrong and please contact us if you have any questions about this.
item.dc:creator: Sideview LLC

item.title: Apprise Alert Action - v2.0.3 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Description:
Adds an alert action to Splunk that allows sending a notification using any of the notification services supported by Apprise (Slack, Office 365, syslog, Telegram, Signal, webhook, email + many, many more). For a full list of supported services see https://github.com/caronc/apprise/wiki
item.dc:creator: Michael Clayfield
